name: Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act (statutory construction cases)
summary: UOCAVA requires states to allow uniformed service members and overseas citizens to register and vote absentee in federal elections, with certain minimum protections for timely ballot submission.
significance: UOCAVA is the central federal statute at issue, requiring states to provide absentee voting opportunities to military and overseas citizens and forming the basis for the respondents' preemption argument.
